title: Add kpod import command
body:
Imports a tarball and saves it as filesystem image.
One error message change to kpod history
Vendor in latest containers/image

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: @rhatdan PTAL
I think you need to redo vendoring the update c/image. A lot more than just containers/image was updated.
@mheon I just did containers/image because I needed the new tarball transport that was added. What else should I update in the vendor?

@umohnani8 The problem is that you picked up too much, not too little - the kubernetes and CNI vendors also updated. I think you need to specifically update only the containers/image vendor with vndr (specify the repo and updated commit directly to vndr). Alternatively, you could directly revert the changes to all the vendors except containers/image by directly manipulating the vendor folder.
@mheon thant is what I did. I changed the commit id of containers/image to match the tarball commit and just vendored that it. I can try it again.

@umohnani8 A few of the other vendors (kubernetes, CNI to name a few) don't have specific commits IDs attached, so they have a tendency to update whenever we vendor something new in. I think @runcom and @mrunalp are familiar with how to work around this - might want to ask them for help?

So when I do `vndr github.com/containers/image` with the tarball commit id, it shows me a bunch of files have been deleted, which include the Kubernetes and CNI files.
I just selected the containers/image files and committed those.

Vendoring looks fine now, thanks @umohnani8 

I'll do a more thorough review after lunch
thanks for pointing that out @mheon :)
Bunch of pull-related tests seem broken. Unclear if this is the new CI acting up or an actual regression.

@mheon the tests should pass now. Something with the new containers/image was causing them to break.
I am not sure what exactly is happening with containers/image right now, but I just vendored in the changes for the tarball transport to support kpod import. @mrunalp @runcom is that acceptable?
Well I like the fact that you got rid of Nalins pool

@umohnani8 Containers/image broke a lot of kpod image-related functions, so you dropped the parts that broke us? I'm a bit concerned that we're broken on the upstream code - we should try and figure out how to fix either c/image or how we use c/image so we can use all the updated code.

@mheon I don't believe I dropped anything that we already had. I just added the tarball folder and changes to the transports folder. There were other changes to containers/image, which was breaking our tests so I didn't commit those after vendoring in containers/image. But does libpod have to be updated with latest containers/image at all times?
@umohnani8 I'm just a little iffy on cherry picking a partial new commit from c/image. I think we should bother someone on the image side to ask why the new commit broke kpod.

@mheon the main thing that was breaking was pulling a docker-archive file - it kept throwing the error "blob size mismatch". There was a PR in containers/image that got merged when it shouldn't have and I spoke to @nalind, but he said most of the changes to that was for containers-storage, so weird that docker-archive is messing up.
I can pull in the changes and try to get to the bottom of why its causing pulling from docker-archive to fail.
@umohnani8 If it's not too much trouble, sure - I'd just like to make sure we aren't going to hit this again next time we have to update c/image.
I agree with Matt, I would rather get this cleaned up in containers/image then get kpod import in.

One last nit, LGTM

@mheon @mitrmac PTAL
@umohnani8 The code from Nalin's c/image fork is going to land upstream at some point, right? I don't want to be permanently tied to his fork instead of upstream
@mheon I believe so. Also Nalin's fork is what has been vendored in for cri-o.
@rhatdan tests passed.

@mheon PTAL
LGTM other then the comment above.
LGTM. @umohnani8 Can you add an issue after this merges to switch back to containers/image master once Nalin's changes merge?
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit c92c997 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan c92c99700c666b276367c9cc6092ef07abc60564 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
